query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Irony', 'Use of words to convey meaning opposite the literal meaning', 'The author hoped the new policy would irony tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irradiance', 'Incident flux of radiant energy per unit area', 'Her irradiance became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irradiate', 'To throw rays of light upon, to illuminate, to brighten', 'Critics argued that the plan might irradiate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irrational', 'Not rational, unfounded or nonsensical', 'What seemed irrational at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irreducible', 'Not able to be reduced or lessened', 'The author hoped the new policy would irreducible tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irrefragable', 'Which cannot be refuted, indisputable, clearly right', 'The passage presents a irrefragable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irrefrangible', 'Not to broken or transgressed, inviolable', 'Critics argued that the plan might irrefrangible the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irrefutable', 'Impossible to deny or disprove; indisputable', 'Critics argued that the plan might irrefutable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irrelevant', 'Not related, not applicable, unimportant, not connected', 'What seemed irrelevant at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irreligious', 'Contrary to religious beliefs and practices', 'Critics argued that the plan might irreligious the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irreparable', 'Incapable of being repaired, amended, cured or rectified', 'The passage presents a irreparable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irreplaceable', 'Impossible to replace or substitute, typically because of unique qualities or value', 'The author hoped the new policy would irreplaceable tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irrepressible', 'Not containable or controllable', 'The passage presents a irrepressible portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irreproachable', 'Beyond criticism or fault; perfect or faultless in behavior or actions', 'The passage presents a irreproachable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irresistible', 'Not able to be resisted', 'Over time, the community learned to irresistible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irresolute', 'Undecided or unsure how to act', 'Critics argued that the plan might irresolute the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irresponsible', 'Lacking a sense of responsibility', 'Her irresponsible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irreverence', 'Lack of respect for', 'The essay highlights the irreverence of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irreverent', 'Lacking proper respect or seriousness, sarcastic', 'The passage presents a irreverent portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irreversible', 'Incapable of being reversed or turned about or back', 'Her irreversible became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irrevocable', 'Unable to be retracted or reversed, final', 'Critics argued that the plan might irrevocable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irrhetorical', 'Not rhetorical', 'The passage presents a irrhetorical portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irrigate', 'To supply farmland with water', 'The author hoped the new policy would irrigate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irritable', 'Capable of being irritated', 'What seemed irritable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irritancy', 'The quality of being irritant or irritating', 'Scholars noted a striking irritancy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irritate', 'To provoke impatience, anger, or displeasure', 'Critics argued that the plan might irritate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irruption', 'The action of irrupting or breaking into, a violent entry', 'The essay highlights the irruption of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Isle', 'An island', 'The essay highlights the isle of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Islet', 'A small island', 'Her islet became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Isobar', 'Atoms having equal weight but different atomis numbers', 'The essay highlights the isobar of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Isochronous', 'Happening at regular intervals', 'What seemed isochronous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Isolate', 'To set apart or cut off from others', 'The author hoped the new policy would isolate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Isothermal', 'Of a process that takes place at constant temperature', 'The essay highlights the isothermal of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Itinerant', 'Traveling from place to place', 'Over time, the community learned to itinerant its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Itinerary', 'A route or proposed route of a journey', 'The essay highlights the itinerary of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Itinerate', 'To travel from place to place', 'Over time, the community learned to itinerate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jad', 'Dulled or satiated by overindulgence', 'Scholars noted a striking jad between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jargon', 'A technical terminology unique to a particular subject', 'Over time, the community learned to jargon its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jaundice', 'To color by prejudice or envy, to prejudice', 'The author hoped the new policy would jaundice tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jaunt', 'To ramble here and there, to stroll, to make an excursion', 'Critics argued that the plan might jaunt the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jeer', 'To utter sarcastic or mocking comments', 'Critics argued that the plan might jeer the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jejune', 'Without interest or significance, dull', 'Her jejune became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jeopardy', 'Danger of loss, harm, or failure', 'The essay highlights the jeopardy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jeremiad', 'A prolonged lamentation or mournful complaint', 'Her jeremiad became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jettison', 'To throw or discard something unnecessary', 'Over time, the community learned to jettison its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jingo', 'One who supports policy favouring war', 'Scholars noted a striking jingo between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jingoist', 'The policy or practice of jingoes, bellicose chauvinism', 'Scholars noted a striking jingoist between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jingoistic', 'Extremely patriotic, often aggressively so', 'The passage presents a jingoistic portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jocose', 'Given to or characterized by joking, jesting, humorous', 'The author hoped the new policy would jocose tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Jocular', 'Characterized by, or intended for joking or jesting', 'The reviewer described the tone as jocular, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
